query_normalized: can publicly available data reveal subtle violations of time-reversal symmetry in beta decay? physics
rationale: The paper focuses on lepton number violation and Majorana neutrino masses in the context of neutrinoless double beta decay, which is a distinct physical mechanism from the time-reversal symmetry (T-symmetry) violations the user is asking about. While both topics fall under "physics beyond the Standard Model" and beta decay, the specific observable (T-violation vs. lepton number violation) and the underlying theoretical constructs are different, failing the "same-mechanism" or "same-variable" cri
